Edgar Chiu

#### What's great

This is awesome! Multi-agent systems are becoming increasingly critical as companies move from simple AI chatbots to real AI workflows.

What stands out about AgentX is its focus on evaluation, observability, and reliability before deployment. Partners across leading banks and system integrators have shared strong positive feedback after adopting AgentX solutions. [...] #### vs Alternatives

We considered other AI agent frameworks and workflow automation tools, but AgentX stood out because it is built for enterprise deployment, not just agent prototyping. The focus on evaluation, observability, reliability, and multi-agent coordination makes it much more practical for teams that need to run AI agents in production with confidence.

results.title: Canary Mail Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
#### What needs improvement

Ishita Sitapara

AJ - appreciate the thoughtful review. Multi-account speed and unified inbox handling are core to Canary, so I’m glad that part is working for you. You’re also spot on about two gaps: deeper customization and AI output that feels consistently useful (not something you ignore). Since your review we’ve kept iterating with workflow releases like Inbox Zero and reliability updates, and we’re still working to make Copilot more functional in real daily writing. If you can share one example of an AI result you expected but didn’t get (drafting, tone, summary, or reply), it helps us improve the experience.

Alessandro Bencini

#### What's great

#### What needs improvement

Ishita Sitapara [...] #### What needs improvement

Ishita Sitapara

Alessandro - thank you. Cross-device sync and offering a one-time purchase option are two things we care a lot about, so it’s great they stood out for you. We’ve continued adding workflow improvements since then (Inbox Zero being a big one) and we’re still fixing bugs as we go. Your note about intelligent email sorting is a strong one - if you can share what “smart sorting” means for you (priority inbox, smart folders, auto-labeling, newsletters), it helps us build it in the most useful way.

Giampiero Nadali

#### What's great

Ishita Sitapara [...] Giampiero Nadali

#### What's great

Ishita Sitapara

Giampiero - thanks for trying Canary and for the kind words on UI and Apple ecosystem sync. Moving from Apple Mail/Airmail smoothly is exactly the experience we aim for. You’ll also see recent workflow additions like Inbox Zero and ongoing stability improvements as we keep polishing the app. If anything in the trial feels unclear or you hit migration friction, reply here and we’ll help.

Maxwell Valentine

results.content:

12 months ago I discovered Langfuse and I've been a happy user ever since. Langfuse made it super easy for me to find & fix weird edge case responses of LLMs and build a new architecture that is much cheaper and more stable.

#### What's great

cost-effective (3)debugging tools (2)

Report

 11 views

•1 review

I've been using Langfuse from day 1 for my LLM package "tinyllm" and all my LLM apps. It just works and the team is fast and very helpful. Probably the best open-source tool/experience I've ever had.

#### What's great

open source (4)

It's easy to integrate in my workflow and you get a bunch of reports and details that would be impossible to do on your own. I love that they offer free cloud version, at least for now. [...] Report

 16 views

•1 review

Solid observability features combined with TS SDK made Langfuse a go-to solution for bootstrapping new AI products. Previously used LangSmith and Helicone, where Langfuse stands out - pricing plan that favors early-stage products and offers more value for the price.

#### What's great

cost-effective (3)SDK availability (2)LLM observability (9)

Report

 14 views

•1 review

We use Langfuse to monitor our GPT usage - specifically to watch for token usage, monitor hallucinations, and trace through request history when things go wrong. Great product and even greater team! Highly recommend trying Langfuse!

#### What's great

detailed tracing (3)monitoring capabilities (5)

Report

 13 views

•2 reviews [...] Report

•1 review

Langfuse has been indispensable for us in leveraging LLMs. Its detailed tracing provides unrivaled clarity, streamlining our debug times and offering insights into both LLM and non-LLM actions. With seamless integrations, from Langchain to their SDKs, and insightful analytics on cost, feedback, and latency, it's the must-have tool for anyone in the LLM space. A bonus: their commitment to open source means flexibility for developers and is in line with the decentralized nature we love in crypto. Kudos to the Langfuse team!

#### What's great

open source (4)seamless integration (3)insightful analytics (2)SDK availability (2)LLM observability (9)detailed tracing (3)

results.title: Claude Code Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Customization

Report

 57 views

•29 reviews

#### What's great

code generation (13)massive context window (4)complex software tasks (9)ai agents (1)

I've built multiple enterprise apps with Claude Code. Not prototypes — actual production systems with payments, auth, real-time features, the lot. I'm building all day every day and it genuinely keeps up. Most AI coding tools feel like autocomplete with extra steps. Claude Code feels like having a senior dev sitting next to you who actually understands context. It reads your codebase, remembers your patterns, and suggests things that make sense for YOUR project, not generic boilerplate.

#### What needs improvement

memory limitations (1) [...] Codex 3.0 by OpenAICodex 3.0 by OpenAI

I tried most of the alternatives. Cursor is great if you live in the IDE, but I'm a terminal person. Copilot is fine for autocomplete but feels shallow when you ask it to do something real. Aider is lovely and open-source, but I kept running into its limits on bigger reasoning tasks. Cody is solid for searching a giant repo, weak for actually changing things. The direct API is the most flexible and the most painful, because you build all the glue yourself. Gemini/ChatGPT in the browser are nice for thinking out loud, useless for touching code. Claude Code hit the sweet spot for me: smart model, real tools, and a harness that doesn't get in the way.

Ratings

Ease of use

Reliability

Value for money

Customization

Report

 57 views

•29 reviews [...] •2 reviews

Claude Code is the backbone of how I build. For LogStitch, a native macOS and SwiftUI app, I used it to break the work into epics and stories, then set up custom agents, skills, and hooks so routine tasks like linting and reviews ran automatically as I coded. It made shipping a polished Mac app as a solo developer genuinely feasible, and it's part of my daily workflow now.

#### Alternatives Considered

Codex 3.0 by OpenAICodex 3.0 by OpenAI
